sleep.title: Sleep
sleep.hrv_note_excellent: Excellent recovery. Your nervous system is well-balanced.
sleep.hrv_note_moderate: Moderate recovery. Consider light activity today.
sleep.hrv_note_poor: Low HRV. Prioritize rest and stress reduction.
sleep.spo2_slightly_low: Slightly below normal. Monitor over the next few nights.
sleep.spo2_very_low_note: Very low. Consider consulting a physician.
sleep.spo2_sync_hint: Sync your ring to see overnight SpO2 data.
sleep.tip_great_1: ⭐ Great sleep! Your consistent schedule is paying off.
sleep.tip_great_2: 💪 Recovery looks solid — you're ready for today's activity.
sleep.tip_improve_1: 💤 Maintain a consistent sleep schedule, even on weekends
sleep.tip_improve_2: 🌙 Create a relaxing bedtime routine 30–60 minutes before sleep
sleep.tip_improve_3: 📱 Avoid screens at least 1 hour before bedtime
sleep.insight_default: Your deep sleep was above average last night. Deep sleep is crucial for physical recovery and memory consolidation.
sleep.nap_contributed: {{minutes}} min of nap time counted toward recovery

sleep_debt.not_enough_data: Need {{count}} more nights of data to calculate sleep debt
sleep_debt.recovery_moderate: Try going to bed 30 min earlier this week
sleep_debt.recovery_high: Prioritize 9+ hours for the next few nights
sleep_debt.insight_none: Your sleep is on target. Keep maintaining your consistent schedule to stay well-rested.
sleep_debt.insight_low: You have a small sleep deficit. Adding 15–20 minutes of extra sleep tonight can help you catch up.
sleep_debt.insight_moderate: Your sleep debt is building up. Try going to bed 30 minutes earlier this week to recover.
sleep_debt.insight_high: Significant sleep debt detected. Prioritize 9+ hours of sleep for the next few nights to support recovery.

nutrition.title: Nutrition
nutrition.description: Track your meals, macros, and nutrition goals. Get personalized insights based on your activity and sleep data.
nutrition.feature_calories_desc: Log meals and track daily intake
nutrition.feature_macros_desc: Set protein, carb, and fat targets

activity.title: Activity
activity.no_workouts_hint: Connect Strava to sync your activities
activity.no_sessions_hint: Sessions will appear after sport-mode sync

focus.title: Focus
focus.sync_cta: Sync your ring to see readiness
focus.sync_body: Pull down to refresh after wearing your ring overnight. We'll calculate your readiness from last night's sleep, HRV, and resting heart rate.
focus.building_body: We're learning your body's patterns. After {{days}} more day{{plural}} of ring data, we'll show you a personalised readiness score.
focus.go_headline: Your body is primed and ready
focus.easy_headline: A lighter effort will serve you better today
focus.rest_headline: Your body is asking for a rest day
focus.go_body_suffix: recovery signals look strong across the board. This is a good day to train hard or push a new effort.
focus.easy_generic: Some recovery signals are slightly off your norm. Moderate activity is fine, but avoid maximal efforts until your scores improve.
focus.rest_generic: Multiple recovery signals are flagged. Skip intense training today -- prioritise sleep, nutrition, and light movement.

readiness.building_body: We need a few days of ring data to calculate individual component scores (HRV, sleep, resting HR). Keep wearing your ring and they'll appear here automatically.
readiness.confidence_one: Based on {{count}} day of your data
readiness.confidence_other: Based on {{count}} days of your data
readiness.empty_sync: Sync your ring to see today's readiness

illness_watch.empty_sync: Sync your ring to see illness signals

last_run.no_strava: Connect Strava to see your run context and effort analysis.
last_run.no_runs: No recent runs found. Go for a run!

strava.sign_in_required: Please sign in to connect Strava
strava.connect_description: Sync your workouts and activities from Strava to get a complete picture of your fitness and training load.
strava.alert_disconnect_message: Are you sure you want to disconnect your Strava account?
strava.sync_up_to_date: Activities up to date. Fetching details…
strava.alert_sync_failed_message: Could not reach Strava. Check your connection and try again.
strava.alert_connect_error_message: An error occurred while connecting to Strava

auth.error_password_short: Password must be at least 6 characters
auth.alert_reset_sent: Check your email for a password reset link
auth.alert_confirm_sent: Check your email to confirm your account

onboarding.subtitle_no_devices: Make sure your ring is charged and nearby

Pairing with {{name}}

onboarding.alert_failed_message: Could not connect to the ring. Please make sure it's nearby and try again.
onboarding.alert_error_message: An error occurred while connecting. Please try again.

hr_live.error_message: Measurement failed. Ensure ring is worn.

hr_daily.empty_hint: Data will appear after the ring syncs

battery.alert_low_10_body: Ring battery is {{battery}}%. Please charge soon.
battery.alert_low_20_body: Ring battery is {{battery}}%. Consider charging your ring.

explainer.recovery_score_subtitle: How ready your body is today
explainer.recovery_score_body: Recovery score combines HRV, resting heart rate, sleep quality, and body temperature to estimate how well your body recovered overnight. A high score means you can push hard today. A low score suggests prioritizing rest.
explainer.sleep_score_subtitle: Overall quality of last night's sleep
explainer.sleep_score_body: Sleep score reflects total sleep duration, time in each stage (deep, REM, light), sleep consistency, and restlessness. It ranges from 0–100 and gives you a single number to gauge how restorative your sleep was.
explainer.strain_body: Strain measures how much cardiovascular stress your body accumulated during the day based on heart rate data. Higher strain means more exertion. Balancing strain against your recovery score helps avoid overtraining.
explainer.readiness_subtitle: Balance of recent strain vs. recovery
explainer.readiness_body: Readiness combines your recent training load with overnight recovery to estimate whether your body is prepared for intense effort. It acts as a guide for how aggressively to push workouts or activities today.
explainer.sleep_duration_body: Sleep duration is the total time you spent asleep, not just in bed. Adults generally need 7–9 hours. Consistently sleeping less than 6 hours raises health risks, while over 10 hours may indicate illness or excessive fatigue.
explainer.sleep_deep_subtitle: Deep, REM, light, and awake time
explainer.sleep_deep_body: Your sleep cycles through light, deep (slow-wave), and REM stages. Deep sleep restores the body physically; REM consolidates memory and emotion. Light sleep bridges the transitions. Awake time should be minimal.
explainer.sleep_rem_body: REM (Rapid Eye Movement) sleep is when most dreaming occurs. It plays a critical role in memory formation, creativity, and emotional regulation. Adults typically spend 20–25% of the night in REM across 4–5 cycles.
explainer.sleep_light_body: Light sleep (N1 and N2 stages) accounts for about half your night. It helps consolidate memories and is a transition into deeper sleep. While less restorative than deep sleep, it is a necessary part of healthy sleep architecture.
explainer.sleep_awake_body: Brief awakenings are normal — you may wake briefly dozens of times without remembering. More than 5% of your sleep window spent awake can reduce overall sleep quality and indicate restlessness or disturbances.
explainer.resting_hr_subtitle: Beats per minute while at rest
explainer.resting_hr_body: Resting heart rate (RHR) is the number of times your heart beats per minute while you're at rest. Lower RHR generally indicates better cardiovascular fitness. An elevated RHR can signal stress, illness, or insufficient recovery.
explainer.live_hr_body: Live heart rate measures your heart rate in real time using your ring's optical sensor. Use this during a workout warm-up, after waking, or to check stress levels. Values vary significantly with activity, stress, and body position.
explainer.daily_hr_body: This chart shows how your heart rate changed throughout the day. Peaks typically coincide with activity or stress, while valleys reflect rest and recovery. Consistent, predictable patterns suggest good cardiovascular health.
explainer.hrv_sdnn_body: Heart Rate Variability (HRV) measures the variation in time between heartbeats. Higher HRV generally indicates better recovery, resilience to stress, and cardiovascular fitness. SDNN is the most common HRV metric from wearables.
explainer.spo2_body: SpO2 (blood oxygen saturation) measures the percentage of hemoglobin in your blood that is carrying oxygen. Healthy adults typically read 95–100%. Values below 90% may indicate a medical concern.
explainer.respiratory_body: Respiratory rate is how many breaths you take per minute during sleep. It's calculated from your ring's heart rate sensor. A normal resting rate is 12–20 breaths per minute. Elevated rates can indicate illness, stress, or sleep apnea.
explainer.body_temp_body: Your ring measures wrist skin temperature, which correlates with core body temperature. Deviations from your personal baseline of ±0.5°C are normal; deviations greater than 1–2°C may indicate fever, illness, or hormonal shifts.
explainer.steps_body: Active calories (also called exercise calories) are the calories your body burns beyond its basal metabolic rate due to physical movement. The daily goal of 500–700 kcal is a good target for general health and body composition.
explainer.calorie_deficit_body: Calorie deficit shows the gap between energy consumed (food) and energy expended (metabolism + activity). A moderate deficit of 200–500 kcal/day supports gradual, sustainable fat loss. Avoid large deficits — they impair recovery and muscle maintenance.
explainer.sleep_trend_7d_subtitle: Your sleep pattern over the past week
explainer.sleep_trend_7d_body: The 7-day trend shows your sleep score each night over the past week. Consistency matters as much as individual nights — irregular schedules disrupt your circadian rhythm, which affects energy, mood, and metabolic health.
explainer.sleep_debt_body: Sleep debt is the total amount of sleep you've missed compared to your target over the past 7 nights. Unlike a bank account, you can't 'pre-sleep' — surplus sleep on one night doesn't cancel out deficit from another. Chronic sleep debt impairs cognition, mood, immune function, and athletic performance.
explainer.contributor_hrv_subtitle: Recent HRV trend vs. your baseline
explainer.contributor_hrv_body: HRV Balance compares your recent (3-day) HRV to your longer-term baseline. A positive balance means your HRV is trending above average — a sign of good recovery accumulation. Negative balance indicates recent stress or under-recovery.
explainer.contributor_rhr_subtitle: Today's resting HR vs. your personal average
explainer.contributor_rhr_body: This contributor compares today's resting heart rate to your 30-day baseline. A resting HR that is 3+ bpm above normal often predicts reduced performance or early illness. Lower-than-normal RHR suggests excellent recovery.
explainer.contributor_sleep_subtitle: Efficiency and depth of last night's sleep
explainer.contributor_sleep_body: Sleep quality is a combined measure of sleep efficiency (time asleep ÷ time in bed), the proportion of deep + REM sleep, and the number of awakenings. It contributes to both recovery and readiness scores.
explainer.contributor_temp_body: Temperature deviation tracks how much your wrist temperature differs from your personal normal. Small fluctuations are expected. A sustained deviation above +1°C may indicate your body is fighting inflammation, infection, or experiencing hormonal changes.
explainer.sleep_baseline_body: Sleep Baseline classifies your average nightly sleep score over the last 14 days into one of four tiers: Low (0–49), Developing (50–64), Good (65–79), and Optimal (80–100). Consistent sleep habits — same bedtime, enough duration, quality environment — gradually lift your tier over weeks.

sleep_baseline.title: Sleep Baseline
sleep_baseline.tip_low_to_developing: Aim for 7–8 hours each night and keep a consistent bedtime.
sleep_baseline.tip_developing_to_good: Reduce screen time before bed and optimise your sleep environment.
sleep_baseline.tip_good_to_optimal: Fine-tune your sleep timing — consistency within 30 minutes each night unlocks peak recovery.
sleep_baseline.at_optimal: Excellent baseline. Keep your current habits to maintain peak sleep quality.

recovery_timeline.title: Recovery today
recovery_timeline.all_optimal: All systems optimal. Great day for a hard session.
recovery_timeline.tip_calib_1: Wear your ring overnight — HRV and sleep are measured while you sleep.
recovery_timeline.tip_calib_2: Sync each morning right after waking up.
recovery_timeline.tip_sleep_low: Sleep is your main drag today — aim for 8h tonight with your ring on.
recovery_timeline.tip_sleep_med: Sleep was below your baseline. Keep effort easy today.
recovery_timeline.tip_sleep_peak: Sleep consistency is key — same bedtime every night lifts your HRV ceiling over weeks.
recovery_timeline.tip_hrv_low: HRV is significantly suppressed — your nervous system needs full rest.
recovery_timeline.tip_hrv_med: HRV is slightly below norm. Skip intensity and prioritize recovery.
recovery_timeline.tip_hrv_peak: To push HRV higher: avoid alcohol, train 80% at low intensity, and keep sleep consistent.
recovery_timeline.tip_rhr_low: Resting HR is elevated — your body is under stress. No hard effort today.
recovery_timeline.tip_rhr_med: Resting HR is slightly high. Keep training moderate.
recovery_timeline.tip_rhr_peak: Resting HR lowers with consistent aerobic base work — more Z2 training over months.
recovery_timeline.tip_load_low: Training load is high relative to your baseline — an easy day will help.
recovery_timeline.tip_load_med: Load is building up. Stay moderate to avoid overreaching.
recovery_timeline.tip_load_peak: Maintain this training rhythm — consistency builds your fitness base and peak readiness.

alerts.find_device_body: Your ring will vibrate if connected
alerts.disconnect_body: Are you sure you want to disconnect from the Smart Ring?
alerts.forget_body: This will remove the paired device from the app. You'll need to scan and reconnect.
alerts.sign_out_body: Are you sure you want to sign out?
alerts.reset_body: This will sign you out and reset all onboarding data.
